name: Gingerbread Baked in Jars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (pan method) or 325°F for jars.
2. Grease a 9 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan or 5 Kerr Quilted Crystal Jelly Jars (#14400-80400). The jars must have straight sides and no bulges. Sterilize the jars by boiling them for 10 minutes, then cool and dry before greasing.
3. In a large bowl, mix flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, salt, ground ginger, cinnamon, and cloves. Stir in margarine, hot water, and molasses until well combined.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan or divide equally among the 5 jars. Place jars on a cookie sheet to prevent tipping.
- Pan method: Bake for 40-50 minutes at 350°F until a cake tester inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool slightly on a wire rack.
- Jar method: Bake for 35-40 minutes at 325°F until a cake tester inserted deep into each jar comes out clean.
5. Once removed from the oven, place lids (hot) on jars using heavy-duty mitts. Tighten lids and replace rings.
6. Allow jars to cool completely on the counter. A "plinking" sound indicates sealing. If no sound is heard, press down on the lid; it should not move.
7. Decorate jars with cotton, decorative cloth, and hot glue gun as desired. Add dried flowers or ribbons for further decoration. Enjoy spicy and moist gingerbread!
